subtitle: A labourâintensive method that yields complex, earthy flavours.
body:
Kimoto is the traditional yeastâstarter method that predates modern additions of lactic acid. Brewers literally grind the rice mixture with poles to encourage wild lactic acid bacteria, a process that takes weeks but results in sake with deep YUM! and a robust character.

Today, only a handful of breweries still use the full kimoto method, valuing its ability to create distinctive, ageâworthy sake.
